title: fix: clarify Perplexity Sonar search presets
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Renames the high-context preset from `perplexity-deep` to `perplexity-high`; keeps `perplexity-deep` and `sonar-deep` as compatibility aliases.
- Gives the low- and high-context Sonar presets distinct titles without implying Perplexity Deep Research.
- Removes repeated model titles from all Perplexity descriptions and records the description-style rule in `AGENTS.md`.
- Keeps upstream routing, pinned search context, pricing, billing, paid status, and provider configuration unchanged.
- Adds catalog and alias/transform regression coverage.

Validation:
- Gen: 11 files, 145 tests passed
- Enter: 3 files, 276 tests passed
- Gen and Enter TypeScript checks passed
- Local `perplexity-high` smoke test returned 200 and billed the high-context request rule

gist.headline: Sonar names find their way
gist.blurb: The Perplexity Sonar presets have received clearer little signposts. High-context search is now called `perplexity-high`, while the old paths still lead home.
gist.summary: Renamed the high-context Perplexity Sonar preset from `perplexity-deep` to `perplexity-high`, retained `perplexity-deep` and `sonar-deep` as compatibility aliases, and clarified the catalog titles and descriptions.
gist.impact: Users can now distinguish low- and high-context Sonar search presets without confusing them for Perplexity Deep Research, while existing alias-based requests continue to route and bill exactly as before.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy model garden has two clearly labeled Sonar search paths: a small low-context trail and a taller high-context trail, with old wooden alias signposts still pointing safely to the high-context path. Polly acts as the observant trail guide, using a tiny explorer hat and map beside a friendly retro terminal showing clean search results.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with black oval eyes, pink rectangular blush, light blue wings, and a tiny explorer hat, gently replacing a garden sign from `perplexity-deep` to `perplexity-high` while two smaller old signposts read `perplexity-deep` and `sonar-deep` and point to the same glowing high-context code-plant; a retro terminal shows tidy Sonar search results among flowers and vines. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with dark purple (#110518) pixel-font labels and a nostalgic, emotionally warm atmosphere.
